name: Threats
description: The Lookout spots servants of the Shadow moving through the landscape — orcs, trolls, wargs, worse. These are not casual encounters; the fellowship has seen something meant to be unseen, or been seen by something that should not see them.

orcs.label: Orcs
orcs.description: Orc-kind on the move — a scouting party, a patrol, a war-band.

events.label: A small orc-scouting party — three or four, moving fast and low, carrying signals. They have not yet seen the fellowship.
events.on_eye: The scouts have already noted the fellowship's tracks and are circling back.
events.notes: Stealth event. Letting them pass is wise; engaging draws the larger force.

events.label: An orc patrol — a dozen or so, armoured and purposeful. They hold the road.
events.on_eye: A second patrol is behind the fellowship, closing the trap.
events.notes: Serious threat. Flight or hiding; direct combat is last resort.

events.label: A war-band of orcs — dozens strong, moving with purpose, banners and drums. They do not know the fellowship exists; best it stays that way.
events.on_eye: The fellowship is spotted; pursuit follows.
events.notes: Flight event. Combat is suicide; the goal is to remain unseen.

events.label: Orcs driving captives — men, women, Dwarves, taken somewhere terrible. The fellowship must decide whether to intervene.
events.on_eye: The captors are more numerous than first glimpsed; rescue is a desperate act.
events.notes: Moral event. Rescue is heroic; passage is grim wisdom.

trolls.label: Trolls
trolls.description: Trolls — stone-skinned, slow-witted, dangerous. They range at night and hide from the sun.

events.label: Stone trolls caught by day, frozen where the sunlight found them. Harmless now, but others of their kin may live nearby.
events.notes: Atmospheric/lore event. The trolls' cache may yield treasure; their living kin are a future threat.

events.label: Trolls on the night road — the thud of heavy feet, the smell of them on the wind. They hunt for food; they have not yet caught the fellowship's scent.
events.on_eye: One of the fellowship is already in their line of sight.
events.notes: Stealth-till-dawn event. Combat is a last resort; sunlight saves.

events.label: A troll-lair spotted in a cave or ravine — piles of bones, the stench of old kills. The trolls are probably within, sleeping through the day.
events.on_eye: Wake the trolls, or worse, find they are not alone.
events.notes: Tactical choice. Circle wide, or raid the lair for treasure during daylight.

wargs.label: Wargs
wargs.description: Wargs — wolf-kin intelligent beyond animal reason, often bearing orc-riders, hunting in packs.

events.label: A warg-pack scenting the wind — heads up, yellow eyes, unhurried. They know there is prey nearby.
events.on_eye: They have scented the fellowship already.
events.notes: Flight or fight. Wargs are faster than horses over distance.

events.label: Orcs mounted on wargs — a terrible combination of speed, intelligence, and malice. A war-party with purpose, on the trail of something.
events.on_eye: The warg-riders are tracking the fellowship itself.
events.notes: Grave threat. Concealment essential; direct combat is desperate.

events.label: A lone warg — separated from its pack, perhaps wounded, perhaps exiled. Still a lethal beast, still cunning. And wherever one warg walks, more may be near.
events.on_eye: The rest of the pack closes while the fellowship faces the one.
events.notes: Encounter event. Beware the isolation being a trap.

attercops.label: Attercops
attercops.description: Great spiders — kin of the spawn of Ungoliant, descendants of Shelob, dwellers in dark woods and deep caves.

events.label: Web-lines between trees, threading the canopy — evidence that attercops hunt these woods. The webs are fresh.
events.on_eye: A spider drops from the canopy as the fellowship passes beneath.
events.notes: Warning event. Stealth preferred; noise summons.

events.label: Great spiders hunting — the scuttle of many legs in the branches, the glint of eyes in the dimness. Silent work required, or the fellowship becomes prey.
events.on_eye: The fellowship is wrapped in a web before they can react; 1 shadow point from the horror.
events.notes: Horror event. Cold steel and fire cut webs; both draw attention.

events.label: An attercop nest — cocooned prey swinging in the branches, the matron and her brood waiting below. The fellowship has stumbled into a larder.
events.on_eye: The matron takes notice; she is far larger than the rest.
events.notes: Genuine peril. Bypass or burn, but do not linger.

evil_men.label: Evil Men
evil_men.description: Men of the East or the South who have sworn themselves to Shadow — soldiers, emissaries, spies.

events.label: A patrol of Men in dark livery — foreign arms, foreign tongue, a discipline that speaks of training. They mark the fellowship but do not pursue.
events.on_eye: They were watching the fellowship specifically; word of their presence travels.
events.notes: Surveillance event. Implications for the campaign, not combat.

events.label: A single rider of the East — an emissary, an assassin, a spy. They pass, observe, and will report.
events.on_eye: The rider is the bait; confederates are in position already.
events.notes: Mystery/investigation event. Interception possible but risky.

events.label: Raiders of the South — a band of horse-Men, hostile to all who hold the old alliances. They charge any who look like the Enemy's enemies.
events.on_eye: Their number is greater than first seen.
events.notes: Combat event. Mounted combat disadvantages foot-travellers.

undead.label: Undead Spirits
undead.description: Wights, barrow-wraiths, restless dead given form by Shadow — the worst kind of threat, against which steel alone may not avail.

events.label: A sudden unnatural chill, the light dimming — a wight moves abroad. It has not yet formed, not yet fixed upon the fellowship.
events.on_eye: The wight solidifies and fixes upon one of the fellowship; shadow points and fear.
events.notes: Supernatural encounter. Hope and song are weapons here.

events.label: A shape walks from a barrow-mound in the twilight — a wight in its proper form, cold and ancient and hungry. The fellowship is between it and living flesh.
events.on_eye: More wights rise; the field behind the fellowship fills with grey shapes.
events.notes: Grave shadow event. Flight or desperate stand; call on Elven-grace.

events.label: A voice on the wind where no speaker stands — a lament, a threat, a name called from nowhere. Someone among the dead remembers something.
events.on_eye: A name in the voice is one of the fellowship's; 1 shadow point.
events.notes: Mystery/shadow event. Respond or flee; engagement has cost.
